description: Charles Schwab is a major U.S. financial services firm offering brokerage, wealth management, banking, custody, asset management, and advisory services to individual investors and independent advisors.

overview.title: Business Profile
paragraphs: Charles Schwab sits at the center of retail investing in the United States, combining self-directed brokerage, advisory programs, banking, custody, and asset management under a scale platform. The company serves tens of millions of brokerage accounts and trillions of dollars in client assets, which gives it enormous distribution power in deciding how everyday investors encounter markets.

Its public posture is investor-friendly and price-disciplined, though the business still depends on familiar financial-sector mechanics: client cash, net interest revenue, asset-based fees, and the steady conversion of trust into account balances.

overview.title: Core Products
paragraphs: Schwab Brokerage is the main retail-facing platform for trading, investing, retirement accounts, ETFs, mutual funds, margin, and related investor services. The post-TD Ameritrade integration also made thinkorswim an important part of Schwab's active-trader and education ecosystem.

Schwab Wealth Advisory extends the company into managed portfolios and personalized financial planning for higher-balance clients. The advisory model matters for the registry because it shifts Schwab from being only a tool provider into a gatekeeper for portfolio construction, investment defaults, and long-term financial behavior.

overview.title: Strategic Context
paragraphs: Schwab's recent reporting emphasizes growth in new brokerage accounts, client assets, financial advice, workplace accounts, and banking relationships. The company is also flagging industry shifts around artificial intelligence, digital assets, private markets, and alternative investments, suggesting that its future product surface will not stop at conventional stock-and-fund brokerage.

For Free The World, Schwab is best evaluated as a financial access platform: it lowers some transaction costs for investors while concentrating brokerage infrastructure, custody relationships, cash economics, and advisory influence inside a single institution.
